dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: Strong: _BaseBM25 base class enforces API parity across 4 variants, each ~50 lines. Protocol types (ScorerProtocol, RerankerProtocol, EmbeddingCallback) define clear interfaces. resolve_scorer centralises scorer resolution. Remaining: HybridSearch internal complexity could be decomposed further.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Much improved: search.py ~750 lines. _types.py centralises types, protocols, and shared helpers. No more duplicated _search_query or scorer resolution. Remaining: search.py still mixes BM25 + HybridSearch + Reranker.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean architecture: base class pattern, Protocol-based interfaces, centralised type system. _types.py serves as the single source of truth for shared concerns. Remaining: HybridSearch and Reranker could be in separate modules.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Greatly improved: resolve_scorer eliminates 4× duplicated scorer resolution in process.py. _search_query consolidated. _blend_reranked_scores extracted. _init_common avoids repetition. Clean, DRY code throughout.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Rust algorithms well-optimized. levenshtein_editops_multiword still allocates inside per-char loop (could hoist). Python code clean with proper error handling and Protocol types.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Significantly improved: _types.py is the single source for Result, MetaResult, Protocol types, resolve_scorer, and _search_query. No more cross-module duplication. engine.py, search.py, process.py, query.py all import from _types.py.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Much improved: Protocol types (ScorerProtocol, RerankerProtocol, EmbeddingCallback) replace raw Any for key interfaces. resolve_scorer has clear return type annotation. Self used for from_column. Remaining: some internal Rust index objects still typed as Any.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: Improved: from_column now uses Self return type. Protocol types define clear interfaces. Remaining: some internal methods could use Protocol types for parameters.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Solid: explain() raises descriptive ValueError. RuntimeError for missing index in engine.py. Import errors chain properly with from e. Consistent pattern throughout.
dimension_notes.naming_quality: Good: _BaseBM25, resolve_scorer, ScorerProtocol all follow Python conventions. Protocol types use clear PascalCase. WRatio/QRatio PascalCase outliers remain for backward compat only.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Greatly improved: resolve_scorer eliminates the 4× duplicated scorer resolution pattern. Each function now calls resolve_scorer(scorer) instead of repeating 3 lines. _search_query is a single-point-of-truth.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: add_documents/remove_documents still rebuild the entire Rust index. This is inherent to the BM25 data structure and documented implicitly. Cost is O(n) but acceptable for typical corpus sizes.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: WRatio/QRatio PascalCase remains for backward compatibility. BM25Algorithm vs BM25Variant use different casing because they serve different Rust endpoints. Both are documented.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Clean: optional dependencies properly guarded with lazy imports. Import error chains preserved. Protocol types are stdlib-only (no new deps).
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Significantly improved: 66 new parametrized tests (test_bm25_variants.py) verify API parity across all 4 variants. Tests cover scoring, properties, explain, metadata, mutation, and pickle roundtrip. 10 langchain tests added. Total: 610 tests.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: Improved: Protocol types define clear interfaces. BM25Algorithm/BM25Variant naming difference documented. resolve_scorer provides consistent scorer handling.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not directly applicable — library, not service. langchain.py lazy class pattern well-tested.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: Nearly complete: _Result/_MetaResult backward-compat aliases remain in search.py but will be removed in a future major version. All new code uses Result/MetaResult from _types.py.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Good: _types.py centralises shared types and helpers. search.py ~750 lines. process.py clean. Could split search.py into sub-package but current size is within bounds.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Minimal: base class pattern eliminates copy-paste. resolve_scorer eliminates formulaic patterns. Protocol types replace ad-hoc Any annotations.

issues.summary: levenshtein_editops_multiword still allocates vectors inside per-character loop
evidence: new_vp and new_vn allocated inside loop unlike myers_multiword which hoists and swaps

issues.summary: WRatio/QRatio use PascalCase while all other fuzz functions use snake_case
evidence: WRatio and QRatio alongside snake_case functions like partial_ratio, token_sort_ratio
issues.suggestion: Add snake_case aliases (w_ratio, q_ratio) for API consistency while keeping originals for backward compat.

issues.summary: BM25Algorithm (lowercase) and BM25Variant (PascalCase) use different casing for similar concepts
issues.suggestion: Document the distinction (search uses lowercase for CLI, join uses PascalCase for Rust FFI) or unify.

issues.summary: add_documents and remove_documents rebuild the entire Rust index
evidence: _rebuild_index recreates the entire Rust index from scratch
issues.suggestion: Document the rebuild cost in docstrings. Consider batch mutation APIs.

issues.summary: search.py contains BM25 + HybridSearch + Reranker in one module
issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into search/bm25.py, search/hybrid.py, search/reranker.py.

issues.summary: Internal Rust index objects typed as Any
issues.suggestion: Not easily fixable since PyO3 objects don't have Python type stubs.

issues.summary: Some import error handlers use 'from None' losing original traceback
evidence: engine.py has some 'raise ImportError(...) from None'
